{
  "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Abstract filter for retaining or discarding records during log compaction and retention enforcement.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Filtering Two-Level Approach:</strong>\n     * <ol>\n     *   <li><strong>Batch Level:</strong> {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} makes a fast decision\n     *       to DELETE (skip entire batch), RETAIN_EMPTY (keep header only), or proceed to record-level filtering.\n     *       This is efficient for large batches where all records share the same fate (e.g., expired segments).</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Record Level:</strong> {@link #shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)} is called for each\n     *       record in batches that were not DELETE-d, allowing fine-grained retention control.</li>\n     * </ol>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Common Implementations:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li><strong>Log Compaction:</strong> Delete old versions of compacted keys, retain latest version.</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Retention Enforcement:</strong> Delete records older than retention time/size.</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Segment Cleanup:</strong> Delete all records in expired segments.</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Delete Horizon Semantics:</strong>\n     * For records with tombstone values (no value) or transaction abort markers, the filter can set a\n     * delete horizon timestamp ({@code currentTime + deleteRetentionMs}) to control when these\n     * cleanup records are themselves removed. This delays deletion to allow consumers to see deletions.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong> Not thread-safe; each thread should use its own filter instance.\n     */\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        /**\n         * Current time in milliseconds, used for timestamp-based retention decisions\n         * (e.g., \"delete records older than this + configured retention\").\n         */\n        public final long currentTime;\n\n        /**\n         * Retention time in milliseconds for delete/abort markers. Used to compute delete horizon\n         * as {@code currentTime + deleteRetentionMs}.\n         */\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        /**\n         * Create a filter with the given time context for retention decisions.\n         *\n         * @param currentTime Current time in milliseconds (typically System.currentTimeMillis())\n         * @param deleteRetentionMs Retention time for deletion records (tombstones and abort markers)\n         */\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }",
  "oldString": "    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * In-memory collection of Kafka record batches backed by a ByteBuffer.\n *\n * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong>\n * This class manages a contiguous buffer containing one or more {@link RecordBatch} objects,\n * providing iteration, filtering, slicing, and serialization capabilities. It is used throughout\n * Kafka for handling batches in producers, consumers, brokers, and administrative operations.\n *\n * <p><strong>Use Cases:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading Existing Batches:</strong> Use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)} to wrap\n *       an existing buffer for reading.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Creating New Batches:</strong> Use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)}\n *       or {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for batch construction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Log Filtering & Compaction:</strong> Use {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}\n *       to filter records by retention policy during log compaction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Network I/O:</strong> Use {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel)} or\n *       {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)} for efficient data transmission.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Structure:</strong>\n * The buffer contains a sequence of batches (one or more). Each batch encapsulates records with shared\n * metadata (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.). Batches are decoded lazily as they are\n * iterated; use {@link #batches()} to iterate over all batches or individual methods like\n * {@link #firstBatchSize()} to inspect specific batches.\n *\n * <p><strong>Decompression & Iterator Semantics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Streaming Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n *       defers decompression until records are consumed, reducing memory footprint.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Regular Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#iterator()} decompresses the full batch\n *       upfront, allowing iteration without resource management concerns.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} combines\n *       batch and record filtering, supporting log compaction and retention policies.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same MemoryRecords instance concurrently.\n * For concurrent access, wrap each thread's iteration with external synchronization or use\n * thread-local instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Buffer Ownership & Lifecycle:</strong>\n * MemoryRecords does not own the backing ByteBuffer; the caller is responsible for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Keeping the buffer valid during MemoryRecords' lifetime</li>\n *   <li>Releasing/deallocating the buffer when no longer needed</li>\n *   <li>Avoiding modifications to the buffer while iteration is in progress</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Batch Parsing:</strong> Batches are decoded on-demand during iteration.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Valid Bytes Caching:</strong> {@link #validBytes()} is computed once and cached;\n *       invalidate by creating a new MemoryRecords instance if the buffer changes.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Decompression Buffers:</strong> Provide reusable {@link BufferSupplier} instances\n *       to streaming iterators to reduce allocations for large compressed batches.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the batch interface\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for constructing new MemoryRecords\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for the V2+ batch implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records (including partial/incomplete batches) to the given channel.\n     *\n     * <p>This method writes the entire buffer contents to the channel, including any partial batches\n     * at the end. It is a lower-level variant of {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)}\n     * that does not skip incomplete data.\n     *\n     * <p>The method handles partial writes by repeatedly calling {@link java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel#write(ByteBuffer[])}\n     * until all data is written. The buffer position is preserved via mark/reset.\n     *\n     * @param channel The target GatheringByteChannel to write to\n     * @return The total number of bytes written to the channel\n     * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the total number of bytes occupied by complete, valid record batches.\n     *\n     * <p>This may be smaller than {@link #sizeInBytes()} if the buffer contains partial or incomplete\n     * batches at the end (e.g., during network transmission or incomplete writes).\n     * The result is cached after the first invocation; it does not reflect changes to the underlying\n     * buffer.\n     *\n     * @return The number of valid bytes representing complete batches\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Peek at the size of the first batch in the buffer without fully parsing it.\n     *\n     * <p>This method validates the batch header up to and including the magic byte, then reads\n     * the batch size field. It is useful for:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Pre-allocating buffers for the first batch</li>\n     *   <li>Detecting incomplete batches during network reads</li>\n     *   <li>Quick validation without full parsing</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * @return The total size of the first batch (including {@link AbstractRecords#LOG_OVERHEAD}\n     *         which is added to the declared batch length), or null if the buffer does not contain\n     *         enough bytes for the header up to the magic byte\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the batch size or magic byte is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter records into a destination buffer, applying batch-level and record-level retention policies.\n     *\n     * <p>This method is the core operation for log compaction and retention enforcement. It iterates\n     * over batches and records, applying a filter to determine which batches/records to retain and\n     * which to discard. Retained records are re-encoded into the destination buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Filtering Semantics:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li><strong>Batch Filtering:</strong> {@link RecordFilter#checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} is called\n     *       first to make a fast decision: DELETE (discard entire batch without inspecting records),\n     *       RETAIN_EMPTY (keep batch header even if all records are deleted), or DELETE_EMPTY (remove\n     *       batch if all records are deleted).</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Record Filtering:</strong> For batches not explicitly deleted, {@link RecordFilter#shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)}\n     *       is called for each record to determine individual retention.</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Batch Rewriting:</strong> If only some records are retained, the batch is re-encoded\n     *       with updated offsets and timestamps while preserving base/last offset/sequence for producer\n     *       state consistency.</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Delete Horizon:</strong>\n     * If the batch contains tombstones or transaction markers and the original batch had no delete\n     * horizon set, the filter assigns a delete horizon timestamp ({@code filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs})\n     * to control when these records are eventually removed.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Buffer Overflow Handling:</strong>\n     * If the filtered output exceeds the destination buffer's capacity, a larger buffer is allocated\n     * internally and returned in the FilterResult. Callers should check {@link FilterResult#outputBuffer()}\n     * and use the returned buffer if different from the input.\n     *\n     * @param filter The retention policy filter; provides batch/record decisions and timing for delete horizons\n     * @param destinationBuffer The initial buffer to write filtered records to (may be enlarged internally)\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier Supplier for decompression buffers. For efficiency with large\n     *                                    compressed batches, provide a supplier that reuses buffers (e.g.,\n     *                                    {@link BufferSupplier} with pooling) rather than allocating fresh\n     *                                    64 KB buffers for each batch.\n     * @return A {@link FilterResult} containing the output buffer, metrics (messagesRead, messagesRetained,\n     *         bytesRead, bytesRetained, maxOffset, maxTimestamp), and the shallow offset of max timestamp\n     *         for use in stream position calculations\n     * @see RecordFilter for implementing custom retention policies\n     * @see FilterResult for interpreting the output metrics\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n            long maxOffset = -1;\n            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n            // Convert records with old record versions\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iterator.next();\n                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n\n                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n\n                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n\n                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n                        containsTombstones = true;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n                }\n            }\n            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n        private final long maxOffset;\n        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n                                  final long maxOffset) {\n            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get a duplicate of the backing ByteBuffer for read-only access.\n     *\n     * <p>Returns a new duplicate with independent position/limit, so modifications to the\n     * returned buffer's position do not affect this MemoryRecords' state. The underlying\n     * buffer data is shared; any changes to the original buffer's contents will be visible\n     * through the returned duplicate.\n     *\n     * @return A duplicate of the backing ByteBuffer\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a new MemoryRecords instance for a contiguous slice of this buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>The returned MemoryRecords wraps a view of the same underlying byte data, reducing memory\n     * overhead vs. copying. It can be used independently to iterate over a subset of batches.\n     * The sliced buffer shares the underlying byte array, so modifications to the original buffer's\n     * contents will be visible through the slice.\n     *\n     * @param position The byte offset within this buffer where the slice should start\n     * @param size The maximum number of bytes to include in the slice\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance backed by a sliced view of the buffer\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if position is negative, exceeds buffer limit, or size is negative\n     */\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n            if (maxOffset < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n        }\n\n        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n            return outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRead() {\n            return messagesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRead() {\n            return bytesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRetained() {\n            return messagesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRetained() {\n            return bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public long maxOffset() {\n            return maxOffset;\n        }\n\n        public long maxTimestamp() {\n            return maxTimestamp;\n        }\n\n        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a MemoryRecords instance for reading batches from an existing buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>The buffer is assumed to contain valid Kafka record batches. Batches are decoded lazily\n     * when iterating. The caller retains ownership of the buffer and is responsible for keeping\n     * it valid and eventually releasing it.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer containing record batches to read\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance wrapping the buffer\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a builder for constructing a new MemoryRecords instance with default parameters.\n     *\n     * <p>Convenience method that uses the current (latest) magic version and CREATE_TIME timestamp type.\n     * For more control over format and behavior, use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long)}.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write records to\n     * @param compression The compression type to apply to records (Compression.NONE, Compression.GZIP, etc.)\n     * @param timestampType The timestamp type for records (usually CREATE_TIME)\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in this batch\n     * @return A new MemoryRecordsBuilder for appending records\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a builder with an explicit maximum size limit.\n     *\n     * <p>The builder will stop accepting records when the total size reaches maxSize.\n     * For LOG_APPEND_TIME timestamp type, the log append time is set to the current system time.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write records to\n     * @param compression The compression type\n     * @param timestampType The timestamp type\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record\n     * @param maxSize The maximum size in bytes this builder is allowed to write\n     * @return A new MemoryRecordsBuilder with the size limit\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               int maxSize) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, baseOffset,\n                RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n                logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional,\n                                               boolean isControlBatch,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n                logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlBatch, partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                buffer.remaining());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a MemoryRecords instance with the given records in a single batch.\n     *\n     * <p>Convenience method using default parameters: magic version 2 (current), CREATE_TIME timestamps,\n     * and base offset 0. For more control, use one of the other {@code withRecords} variants or\n     * {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder}.\n     *\n     * @param compression The compression type for the batch\n     * @param records The records to include (SimpleRecord instances are easier to construct than Record)\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance containing the records in a single batch\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n                partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a MemoryRecords instance with idempotent producer records.\n     *\n     * <p>Idempotent batches carry producer ID and base sequence number for deduplication.\n     * This is used by producers with exactly-once-per-batch semantics. Base offset is 0\n     * and partition leader epoch is unknown (NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH).\n     *\n     * @param compression The compression type for the batch\n     * @param producerId The producer ID\n     * @param producerEpoch The producer epoch (for fencing stale producers)\n     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number (0 for first batch from producer)\n     * @param records The records to include\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance with idempotent batch semantics\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                                      long producerId, short producerEpoch, int baseSequence,\n                                                      int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                      SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a MemoryRecords instance with transactional producer records.\n     *\n     * <p>Transactional batches carry producer ID, epoch, and sequence number, and are marked as\n     * part of a transaction. The broker will ensure all-or-nothing delivery and appropriate\n     * isolation semantics. Base offset is 0 and partition leader epoch is unknown.\n     *\n     * @param compression The compression type for the batch\n     * @param producerId The producer ID\n     * @param producerEpoch The producer epoch\n     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number\n     * @param records The records to include (will be visible only after transaction commits)\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance with transactional batch semantics\n     * @see #withEndTransactionMarker(long, short, EndTransactionMarker) for committing/aborting the transaction\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                                         long producerId, short producerEpoch, int baseSequence,\n                                                         int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, true, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                         SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withTransactionalRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n                false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                            int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n                                            SimpleRecord... records) {\n        if (records.length == 0)\n            return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n        int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferStream, magic, compression, timestampType,\n                initialOffset, logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false,\n                partitionLeaderEpoch, sizeEstimate)) {\n            for (SimpleRecord record : records)\n                builder.append(record);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, System.currentTimeMillis(), RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, marker);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long timestamp, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, timestamp, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, producerId,\n                producerEpoch, marker);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long initialOffset, long timestamp, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                         long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        int endTxnMarkerBatchSize = DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + marker.endTxnMarkerValueSize();\n        ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(endTxnMarkerBatchSize);\n        writeEndTransactionalMarker(buffer, initialOffset, timestamp, partitionLeaderEpoch, producerId,\n                producerEpoch, marker);\n        buffer.flip();\n        return MemoryRecords.readableRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,\n                                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                   EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        boolean isTransactional = true;\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, Compression.NONE,\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, initialOffset, timestamp, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, isTransactional, true, partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                buffer.capacity())\n        ) {\n            builder.appendEndTxnMarker(timestamp, marker);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withLeaderChangeMessage(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        LeaderChangeMessage leaderChangeMessage\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendLeaderChangeMessage(timestamp, leaderChangeMessage);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withSnapshotHeaderRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        SnapshotHeaderRecord snapshotHeaderRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendSnapshotHeaderMessage(timestamp, snapshotHeaderRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withSnapshotFooterRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        SnapshotFooterRecord snapshotFooterRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendSnapshotFooterMessage(timestamp, snapshotFooterRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withKRaftVersionRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        KRaftVersionRecord kraftVersionRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendKRaftVersionMessage(timestamp, kraftVersionRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withVotersRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        VotersRecord votersRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendVotersMessage(timestamp, votersRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer\n    ) {\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(\n            buffer,\n            RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n            Compression.NONE,\n            TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n            initialOffset,\n            timestamp,\n            RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n            RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH,\n            RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false,\n            true,\n            leaderEpoch,\n            buffer.capacity()\n        );\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "         return buffer.hashCode();",
        "     }",
        " ",
        "+    /**",
        "+     * Abstract filter for retaining or discarding records during log compaction and retention enforcement.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><strong>Filtering Two-Level Approach:</strong>",
        "+     * <ol>",
        "+     *   <li><strong>Batch Level:</strong> {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} makes a fast decision",
        "+     *       to DELETE (skip entire batch), RETAIN_EMPTY (keep header only), or proceed to record-level filtering.",
        "+     *       This is efficient for large batches where all records share the same fate (e.g., expired segments).</li>",
        "+     *   <li><strong>Record Level:</strong> {@link #shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)} is called for each",
        "+     *       record in batches that were not DELETE-d, allowing fine-grained retention control.</li>",
        "+     * </ol>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><strong>Common Implementations:</strong>",
        "+     * <ul>",
        "+     *   <li><strong>Log Compaction:</strong> Delete old versions of compacted keys, retain latest version.</li>",
        "+     *   <li><strong>Retention Enforcement:</strong> Delete records older than retention time/size.</li>",
        "+     *   <li><strong>Segment Cleanup:</strong> Delete all records in expired segments.</li>",
        "+     * </ul>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><strong>Delete Horizon Semantics:</strong>",
        "+     * For records with tombstone values (no value) or transaction abort markers, the filter can set a",
        "+     * delete horizon timestamp ({@code currentTime + deleteRetentionMs}) to control when these",
        "+     * cleanup records are themselves removed. This delays deletion to allow consumers to see deletions.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong> Not thread-safe; each thread should use its own filter instance.",
        "+     */",
        "     public abstract static class RecordFilter {",
        "+        /**",
        "+         * Current time in milliseconds, used for timestamp-based retention decisions",
        "+         * (e.g., \"delete records older than this + configured retention\").",
        "+         */",
        "         public final long currentTime;",
        "+",
        "+        /**",
        "+         * Retention time in milliseconds for delete/abort markers. Used to compute delete horizon",
        "+         * as {@code currentTime + deleteRetentionMs}.",
        "+         */",
        "         public final long deleteRetentionMs;",
        " ",
        "+        /**",
        "+         * Create a filter with the given time context for retention decisions.",
        "+         *",
        "+         * @param currentTime Current time in milliseconds (typically System.currentTimeMillis())",
        "+         * @param deleteRetentionMs Retention time for deletion records (tombstones and abort markers)",
        "+         */",
        "         public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {",
        "             this.currentTime = currentTime;",
        "             this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;"
      ],
      "newLines": 51,
      "newStart": 486,
      "oldLines": 10,
      "oldStart": 486
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
  "new_string": "    /**\n     * Result of filtering records through {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}.\n     *\n     * <p>This class encapsulates the output buffer and comprehensive metrics about what was filtered:\n     * how many records were read, how many retained, the bytes consumed and output, and timestamp\n     * information for log position tracking.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Metrics Interpretation:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li><strong>messagesRead:</strong> Total records examined (including discarded ones)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>messagesRetained:</strong> Records written to output (and empty batches with header only)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>bytesRead:</strong> Total bytes examined (all batches, including DELETE-d ones)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>bytesRetained:</strong> Bytes written to output buffer</li>\n     *   <li><strong>maxOffset:</strong> Highest offset in retained records (for log position tracking)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>maxTimestamp:</strong> Maximum timestamp in retained records</li>\n     *   <li><strong>shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp:</strong> Shallow offset (in this filtered set) of max timestamp</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p>These metrics are useful for monitoring compaction progress and validating log integrity.\n     */\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        /**\n         * Construct a filter result with the given output buffer.\n         *\n         * @param outputBuffer The ByteBuffer to which filtered records are written\n         */\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }",
  "old_string": "    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "    /**\n     * Result of filtering records through {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}.\n     *\n     * <p>This class encapsulates the output buffer and comprehensive metrics about what was filtered:\n     * how many records were read, how many retained, the bytes consumed and output, and timestamp\n     * information for log position tracking.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Metrics Interpretation:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li><strong>messagesRead:</strong> Total records examined (including discarded ones)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>messagesRetained:</strong> Records written to output (and empty batches with header only)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>bytesRead:</strong> Total bytes examined (all batches, including DELETE-d ones)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>bytesRetained:</strong> Bytes written to output buffer</li>\n     *   <li><strong>maxOffset:</strong> Highest offset in retained records (for log position tracking)</li>\n     *   <li><strong>maxTimestamp:</strong> Maximum timestamp in retained records</li>\n     *   <li><strong>shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp:</strong> Shallow offset (in this filtered set) of max timestamp</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p>These metrics are useful for monitoring compaction progress and validating log integrity.\n     */\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        /**\n         * Construct a filter result with the given output buffer.\n         *\n         * @param outputBuffer The ByteBuffer to which filtered records are written\n         */\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }",
  "oldString": "    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * In-memory collection of Kafka record batches backed by a ByteBuffer.\n *\n * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong>\n * This class manages a contiguous buffer containing one or more {@link RecordBatch} objects,\n * providing iteration, filtering, slicing, and serialization capabilities. It is used throughout\n * Kafka for handling batches in producers, consumers, brokers, and administrative operations.\n *\n * <p><strong>Use Cases:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading Existing Batches:</strong> Use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)} to wrap\n *       an existing buffer for reading.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Creating New Batches:</strong> Use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)}\n *       or {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for batch construction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Log Filtering & Compaction:</strong> Use {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}\n *       to filter records by retention policy during log compaction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Network I/O:</strong> Use {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel)} or\n *       {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)} for efficient data transmission.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Structure:</strong>\n * The buffer contains a sequence of batches (one or more). Each batch encapsulates records with shared\n * metadata (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.). Batches are decoded lazily as they are\n * iterated; use {@link #batches()} to iterate over all batches or individual methods like\n * {@link #firstBatchSize()} to inspect specific batches.\n *\n * <p><strong>Decompression & Iterator Semantics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Streaming Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n *       defers decompression until records are consumed, reducing memory footprint.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Regular Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#iterator()} decompresses the full batch\n *       upfront, allowing iteration without resource management concerns.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} combines\n *       batch and record filtering, supporting log compaction and retention policies.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same MemoryRecords instance concurrently.\n * For concurrent access, wrap each thread's iteration with external synchronization or use\n * thread-local instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Buffer Ownership & Lifecycle:</strong>\n * MemoryRecords does not own the backing ByteBuffer; the caller is responsible for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Keeping the buffer valid during MemoryRecords' lifetime</li>\n *   <li>Releasing/deallocating the buffer when no longer needed</li>\n *   <li>Avoiding modifications to the buffer while iteration is in progress</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Batch Parsing:</strong> Batches are decoded on-demand during iteration.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Valid Bytes Caching:</strong> {@link #validBytes()} is computed once and cached;\n *       invalidate by creating a new MemoryRecords instance if the buffer changes.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Decompression Buffers:</strong> Provide reusable {@link BufferSupplier} instances\n *       to streaming iterators to reduce allocations for large compressed batches.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the batch interface\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for constructing new MemoryRecords\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for the V2+ batch implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records (including partial/incomplete batches) to the given channel.\n     *\n     * <p>This method writes the entire buffer contents to the channel, including any partial batches\n     * at the end. It is a lower-level variant of {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)}\n     * that does not skip incomplete data.\n     *\n     * <p>The method handles partial writes by repeatedly calling {@link java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel#write(ByteBuffer[])}\n     * until all data is written. The buffer position is preserved via mark/reset.\n     *\n     * @param channel The target GatheringByteChannel to write to\n     * @return The total number of bytes written to the channel\n     * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurs while writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the total number of bytes occupied by complete, valid record batches.\n     *\n     * <p>This may be smaller than {@link #sizeInBytes()} if the buffer contains partial or incomplete\n     * batches at the end (e.g., during network transmission or incomplete writes).\n     * The result is cached after the first invocation; it does not reflect changes to the underlying\n     * buffer.\n     *\n     * @return The number of valid bytes representing complete batches\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Peek at the size of the first batch in the buffer without fully parsing it.\n     *\n     * <p>This method validates the batch header up to and including the magic byte, then reads\n     * the batch size field. It is useful for:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Pre-allocating buffers for the first batch</li>\n     *   <li>Detecting incomplete batches during network reads</li>\n     *   <li>Quick validation without full parsing</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * @return The total size of the first batch (including {@link AbstractRecords#LOG_OVERHEAD}\n     *         which is added to the declared batch length), or null if the buffer does not contain\n     *         enough bytes for the header up to the magic byte\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the batch size or magic byte is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter records into a destination buffer, applying batch-level and record-level retention policies.\n     *\n     * <p>This method is the core operation for log compaction and retention enforcement. It iterates\n     * over batches and records, applying a filter to determine which batches/records to retain and\n     * which to discard. Retained records are re-encoded into the destination buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Filtering Semantics:</strong>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li><strong>Batch Filtering:</strong> {@link RecordFilter#checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} is called\n     *       first to make a fast decision: DELETE (discard entire batch without inspecting records),\n     *       RETAIN_EMPTY (keep batch header even if all records are deleted), or DELETE_EMPTY (remove\n     *       batch if all records are deleted).</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Record Filtering:</strong> For batches not explicitly deleted, {@link RecordFilter#shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)}\n     *       is called for each record to determine individual retention.</li>\n     *   <li><strong>Batch Rewriting:</strong> If only some records are retained, the batch is re-encoded\n     *       with updated offsets and timestamps while preserving base/last offset/sequence for producer\n     *       state consistency.</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Delete Horizon:</strong>\n     * If the batch contains tombstones or transaction markers and the original batch had no delete\n     * horizon set, the filter assigns a delete horizon timestamp ({@code filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs})\n     * to control when these records are eventually removed.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Buffer Overflow Handling:</strong>\n     * If the filtered output exceeds the destination buffer's capacity, a larger buffer is allocated\n     * internally and returned in the FilterResult. Callers should check {@link FilterResult#outputBuffer()}\n     * and use the returned buffer if different from the input.\n     *\n     * @param filter The retention policy filter; provides batch/record decisions and timing for delete horizons\n     * @param destinationBuffer The initial buffer to write filtered records to (may be enlarged internally)\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier Supplier for decompression buffers. For efficiency with large\n     *                                    compressed batches, provide a supplier that reuses buffers (e.g.,\n     *                                    {@link BufferSupplier} with pooling) rather than allocating fresh\n     *                                    64 KB buffers for each batch.\n     * @return A {@link FilterResult} containing the output buffer, metrics
