  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format. This interface abstracts over different\n * versions of the record format (magic versions 0, 1, 2, and higher) and provides access to batch metadata\n * and individual records.\n *\n * <p><strong>Record Format Versions:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Magic V0 & V1:</strong> Older formats where a batch could contain a single record (uncompressed)\n *       or multiple records (compressed). These versions lack producer ID, sequence numbers, and timestamps.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Magic V2+:</strong> Newer formats that support idempotent and transactional producers with sequence numbers,\n *       producer ID/epoch tracking, and explicit timestamp types. These batches support both compressed and\n *       uncompressed records.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Role in Kafka Protocol:</strong>\n * Record batches are the basic unit of data in Kafka logs. They encapsulate a group of records with shared metadata\n * (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.) and provide efficient encoding of records using offset/timestamp\n * deltas relative to batch base values. This design minimizes storage overhead when records share similar offsets and timestamps.\n *\n * <p><strong>Compaction Behavior:</strong>\n * During log compaction, base and last offset/sequence numbers are preserved for V2+ batches to maintain producer\n * state consistency, but individual records may be removed. This allows brokers to rebuild producer state from the log.\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This interface does not mandate thread-safety. Implementations may be mutable (e.g., {@link DefaultRecordBatch})\n * and should not be accessed concurrently from multiple threads without external synchronization.\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for the V2+ concrete implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords for in-memory collections of record batches\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */",
    "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
    "numLines": 80,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 299
  },
  "type": "text"

  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStream;\nimport java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.NoSuchElementException;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n\n/**\n * Concrete implementation of {@link RecordBatch} for Kafka record format magic version 2 and above.\n *\n * <p><strong>Binary Layout:</strong>\n * This class handles the serialization/deserialization of the following binary schema:\n * <pre>\n * RecordBatch =>\n *  BaseOffset => Int64\n *  Length => Int32\n *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n *  Magic => Int8\n *  CRC => Uint32\n *  Attributes => Int16\n *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\n *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n *  ProducerId => Int64\n *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n *  BaseSequence => Int32\n *  RecordsCount => Int32\n *  Records => [Record]\n * </pre>\n *\n * <p><strong>Compression:</strong>\n * When compression is enabled, the record data following the RecordsCount field is compressed as a single block.\n * Decompression is performed lazily when iterating over records via {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n * or eagerly in {@link #iterator()} to avoid resource leaks.\n *\n * <p><strong>CRC Computation:</strong>\n * The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial covers all batch data from the Attributes field onwards (excluding\n * PartitionLeaderEpoch which is not included in CRC computation to avoid recomputation on broker assignment).\n *\n * <p><strong>Compaction & Producer State Preservation:</strong>\n * During log compaction, this batch preserves base and last offset/sequence numbers from the original batch,\n * allowing brokers to rebuild accurate producer state. Empty batches may be retained to preserve producer\n * sequence information until a new sequence is written or the producer ID expires.\n *\n * <p><strong>Timestamp Semantics:</strong>\n * BaseTimestamp represents the timestamp of the first record (or delete horizon for tombstone batches).\n * MaxTimestamp reflects the maximum timestamp of retained records (for CREATE_TIME) or the broker-assigned\n * log append time (for LOG_APPEND_TIME).\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Attributes:</strong>\n * <table border=\"1\">\n *   <tr><th>Bits</th><th>Field</th></tr>\n *   <tr><td>0-2</td><td>Compression Type (0=None, 1=GZIP, 2=Snappy, 3=LZ4, 4=Zstandard)</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>3</td><td>Timestamp Type (0=CreateTime, 1=LogAppendTime)</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>4</td><td>Transactional Flag</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>5</td><td>Control Flag (control batches for transaction markers)</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>6</td><td>Delete Horizon Flag</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>7-15</td><td>Reserved</td></tr>\n * </table>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same DefaultRecordBatch instance concurrently.\n * Thread-safe access requires external synchronization or per-thread instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Decompression:</strong> Use {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} to decompress\n *       records on-demand, reducing memory footprint for large compressed batches.</li>\n *   <li><strong>CRC Computation:</strong> {@link #isValid()} recomputes the CRC every time; cache results\n *       if validating the same batch multiple times.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Iterator Creation:</strong> {@link #iterator()} decompresses the entire batch immediately\n *       for regular iterator support, while {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} defers decompression.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the interface contract\n * @see MemoryRecords for managing multiple batches in memory\n */\npublic class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n    static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n    public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n    static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n    public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n    static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n    public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n    static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n    public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n\n    private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n    private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n    private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n    private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n    private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java",
    "numLines": 150,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 895
  },

  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * In-memory collection of Kafka record batches backed by a ByteBuffer.\n *\n * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong>\n * This class manages a contiguous buffer containing one or more {@link RecordBatch} objects,\n * providing iteration, filtering, slicing, and serialization capabilities. It is used throughout\n * Kafka for handling batches in producers, consumers, brokers, and administrative operations.\n *\n * <p><strong>Use Cases:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading Existing Batches:</strong> Use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)} to wrap\n *       an existing buffer for reading.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Creating New Batches:</strong> Use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)}\n *       or {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for batch construction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Log Filtering & Compaction:</strong> Use {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}\n *       to filter records by retention policy during log compaction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Network I/O:</strong> Use {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel)} or\n *       {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)} for efficient data transmission.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Structure:</strong>\n * The buffer contains a sequence of batches (one or more). Each batch encapsulates records with shared\n * metadata (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.). Batches are decoded lazily as they are\n * iterated; use {@link #batches()} to iterate over all batches or individual methods like\n * {@link #firstBatchSize()} to inspect specific batches.\n *\n * <p><strong>Decompression & Iterator Semantics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Streaming Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n *       defers decompression until records are consumed, reducing memory footprint.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Regular Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#iterator()} decompresses the full batch\n *       upfront, allowing iteration without resource management concerns.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} combines\n *       batch and record filtering, supporting log compaction and retention policies.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same MemoryRecords instance concurrently.\n * For concurrent access, wrap each thread's iteration with external synchronization or use\n * thread-local instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Buffer Ownership & Lifecycle:</strong>\n * MemoryRecords does not own the backing ByteBuffer; the caller is responsible for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Keeping the buffer valid during MemoryRecords' lifetime</li>\n *   <li>Releasing/deallocating the buffer when no longer needed</li>\n *   <li>Avoiding modifications to the buffer while iteration is in progress</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Batch Parsing:</strong> Batches are decoded on-demand during iteration.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Valid Bytes Caching:</strong> {@link #validBytes()} is computed once and cached;\n *       invalidate by creating a new MemoryRecords instance if the buffer changes.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Decompression Buffers:</strong> Provide reusable {@link BufferSupplier} instances\n *       to streaming iterators to reduce allocations for large compressed batches.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the batch interface\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for constructing new MemoryRecords\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for the V2+ batch implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }",

1. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
/**
 * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),
 * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain
 * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records
 * regardless of compression.
 */
public interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {
After
/**
 * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format. This interface abstracts over different
 * versions of the record format (magic versions 0, 1, 2, and higher) and provides access to batch metadata
 * and individual records.
 *
 * <p><strong>Record Format Versions:</strong>
 * <ul>
 *   <li><strong>Magic V0 & V1:</strong> Older formats where a batch could contain a single record (uncompressed)
 *       or multiple records (compressed). These versions lack producer ID, sequence numbers, and timestamps.</li>
 *   <li><strong>Magic V2+:</strong> Newer formats that support idempotent and transactional producers with sequence numbers,
 *       producer ID/epoch tracking, and explicit timestamp types. These batches support both compressed and
 *       uncompressed records.</li>
 * </ul>
 *
 * <p><strong>Role in Kafka Protocol:</strong>
 * Record batches are the basic unit of data in Kafka logs. They encapsulate a group of records with shared metadata
 * (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.) and provide efficient encoding of records using offset/timestamp
 * deltas relative to batch base values. This design minimizes storage overhead when records share similar offsets and timestamps.
 *
 * <p><strong>Compaction Behavior:</strong>
 * During log compaction, base and last offset/sequence numbers are preserved for V2+ batches to maintain producer
 * state consistency, but individual records may be removed. This allows brokers to rebuild producer state from the log.
 *
 * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>
 * This interface does not mandate thread-safety. Implementations may be mutable (e.g., {@link DefaultRecordBatch})
 * and should not be accessed concurrently from multiple threads without external synchronization.
 *
 * @see DefaultRecordB
... (truncated)

     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.

     * Check whether this batch has a valid producer ID set.

     *
     * @return true if the batch has a valid (non-sentinel) producer ID, false otherwise
     */
    boolean hasProducerId();
3. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
/**
 * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:
 *
 * RecordBatch =>
 *  BaseOffset => Int64
 *  Length => Int32
 *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32
 *  Magic => Int8
 *  CRC => Uint32
 *  Attributes => Int16
 *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta
 *  BaseTimestamp => Int64
 *  MaxTimestamp => Int64
 *  ProducerId => Int64
 *  ProducerEpoch => Int16
 *  BaseSequence => Int32
 *  RecordsCount => Int32
 *  Records => [Record]
 *
 * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized
 * directly following the count of the number of records.
 *
 * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is
 * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret
 * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC
 * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by
 * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.
 *
 * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence
 * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the
 * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following
 * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence
 * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an
 * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence e
... (truncated)
After
/**
 * Concrete implementation of {@link RecordBatch} for Kafka record format magic version 2 and above.
 *
 * <p><strong>Binary Layout:</strong>
 * This class handles the serialization/deserialization of the following binary schema:
 * <pre>
 * RecordBatch =>
 *  BaseOffset => Int64
 *  Length => Int32
 *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32
 *  Magic => Int8
 *  CRC => Uint32
 *  Attributes => Int16
 *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta
 *  BaseTimestamp => Int64
 *  MaxTimestamp => Int64
 *  ProducerId => Int64
 *  ProducerEpoch => Int16
 *  BaseSequence => Int32
 *  RecordsCount => Int32
 *  Records => [Record]
 * </pre>
 *
 * <p><strong>Compression:</strong>
 * When compression is enabled, the record data following the RecordsCount field is compressed as a single block.
 * Decompression is performed lazily when iterating over records via {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}
 * or eagerly in {@link #iterator()} to avoid resource leaks.
 *
 * <p><strong>CRC Computation:</strong>
 * The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial covers all batch data from the Attributes field onwards (excluding
 * PartitionLeaderEpoch which is not included in CRC computation to avoid recomputation on broker assignment).
 *
 * <p><strong>Compaction & Producer State Preservation:</strong>
 * During log compaction, this batch preserves base and last offset/sequence numbers from the original batch,
 * allowing brokers to rebuild accurate producer state. Empty batches may be retained to preserve producer
 * sequence information until a new sequence is written or the producer ID expires.
 *
 * <p><strong>Timestamp Semantics:</strong>
 * BaseTimestamp represents the timestamp of the first record (or delete horizon for tombstone batches).
 * MaxTimestamp reflects the maximum t
... (truncated)
4. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used to calculate the record timestamps from the deltas.
     *
     * @return The base timestamp
     */
    public long baseTimestamp() {
        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);
    }
After
    /**
     * Get the base timestamp of this batch, which serves as the reference point for record timestamp deltas.
     *
     * <p>All record timestamps are stored as deltas relative to this base timestamp.
     * If this batch is empty or marked with the delete horizon flag, baseTimestamp may be set to
     * -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP) or the delete horizon time respectively.
     *
     * @return The base timestamp value (may be NO_TIMESTAMP (-1) if the batch is empty or uses delete horizon)
     * @see #maxTimestamp() for the maximum timestamp in the batch
     */
    public long baseTimestamp() {
        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);
    }
5. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    @Override
    public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {
        buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());
    }

    @Override
    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {
        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());
    }
After
    /**
     * Write the entire batch (header and records) to the provided ByteBuffer.
     *
     * <p>This method writes the batch starting at the buffer's current position.
     * The position is advanced by the batch size. This is a shallow copy operation that
     * does not decompress records.
     *
     * @param buffer The target ByteBuffer to write to
     * @throws BufferOverflowException if the buffer has insufficient remaining capacity
     * @see #sizeInBytes() to determine required capacity
     */
    @Override
    public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {
        buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());
    }

    /**
     * Write the entire batch (header and records) to the provided output stream.
     *
     * <p>This method writes the batch to the stream without decompression.
     * The stream's position is advanced by the batch size.
     *
     * @param outputStream The target output stream to write to
     */
    @Override
    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {
        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());
    }
6. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    @Override
    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {
        if (count() == 0) {
            return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());
        }

        /*
         * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since
         * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually
         * as it will allocate new byte array.
         */
        if (!isCompressed())
            return uncompressedIterator();

        // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it
        // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time
        return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);
    }
After
    /**
     * Return a closeable iterator over records that skips the key/value/headers fields for efficiency.
     *
     * <p>This is useful for validation or operations that only need record metadata (offset, timestamp, headers)
     * without accessing the full key/value content. For uncompressed batches, record data is still read but
     * not exposed. For compressed batches, this defers decompression until iteration, saving memory.
     *
     * <p><strong>Caller Responsibility:</strong> The returned iterator must be closed to release decompression resources.
     *
     * @param bufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffers for decompression. For memory efficiency with large
     *                       compressed batches, provide a buffer supplier that reuses buffers.
     * @return A closeable iterator over records (with key/value/headers omitted)
     */
    @Override
    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {
        if (count() == 0) {
            return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());
        }

        /*
         * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since
         * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually
         * as it will allocate new byte array.
         */
        if (!isCompressed())
            return uncompressedIterator();

        // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it
        // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time
        return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);
    }

    /**
     * Write an empty batch header (no records) to a ByteBuffer.
     *
     * <p>This is used to preserve producer state during log compaction when all records in a batch
     * are deleted but the base/last sequence numbers must be retained for duplicate detection.

     * @param magic The record format magic version (must be 2 or higher)

     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number of the (now empty) batch

     * @param timestampType The timestamp type (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)
     * @param timestamp The timestamp value (usually base timestamp or delete horizon)

    /**
     * Write a complete batch header with metadata to a ByteBuffer.
     *
     * <p>This method writes the fixed-size batch header portion (typically 61 bytes for magic V2+)
     * but does not write any record data. Record data should be written immediately after the header
     * at the buffer's current position after this method returns.
     *
     * <p>The method computes and writes the CRC over the attributes field through the end of the
     * record data (which may not yet be in the buffer when this method is called if records are
     * written sequentially).
     *
     * @param buffer The target ByteBuffer positioned at the offset where the batch header should start
     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in this batch
     * @param lastOffsetDelta The delta from base offset to the last record's offset (used to compute last offset)
     * @param sizeInBytes The total size of the batch including header and all records, with the {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD}
     *                    constant subtracted (i.e., the size of the portion after the Length field)
     * @param magic The record format magic version (must be 2 or higher)
     * @param compressionType The compression type applied to records (NONE, GZIP, SNAPPY, LZ4, or ZSTD)
     * @param timestampType The timestamp type for records (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)
     * @param baseTimestamp The timestamp base value (or NO_TIMESTAMP if batch will be empty; or delete horizon if delete horizon flag is set)
     * @param maxTimestamp The maximum timestamp of records (or for LOG_APPEND_TIME, the broker-assigned append time)

12. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {
        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)
            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;
        return sequence + increment;
    }

    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {
        if (sequence < decrement)
            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;
        return sequence - decrement;
    }
After
    /**
     * Increment a sequence number by the given amount, handling wraparound at Integer.MAX_VALUE.
     *
     * <p>Sequence numbers in Kafka are 32-bit signed integers that wrap around.
     * When adding exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE, the result wraps into the negative range and continues.
     * This method handles that wraparound arithmetic correctly.
     *
     * @param sequence The starting sequence number
     * @param increment The amount to add (typically 1 or the number of records in a batch)
     * @return The resulting sequence number after addition with wraparound
     */
    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {
        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)
            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;
        return sequence + increment;
    }

    /**
     * Decrement a sequence number by the given amount, handling wraparound from negative to positive.
     *
     * <p>Sequence numbers wrap around at Integer.MAX_VALUE. When subtracting causes the value to go
     * below Integer.MIN_VALUE (negative values), the result wraps into the positive range.
     * This method handles that wraparound arithmetic correctly.
     *
     * @param sequence The starting sequence number
     * @param decrement The amount to subtract (typically 1 or the number of records in a batch)
     * @return The resulting sequence number after subtraction with wraparound
     */
    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {
        if (sequence < decrement)
            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;
        return sequence - decrement;
    }
13. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
/**
 * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used only for reading or
 * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},
 * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.
 */
public class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {
After
/**
 * In-memory collection of Kafka record batches backed by a ByteBuffer.
 *
 * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong>
 * This class manages a contiguous buffer containing one or more {@link RecordBatch} objects,
 * providing iteration, filtering, slicing, and serialization capabilities. It is used throughout
 * Kafka for handling batches in producers, consumers, brokers, and administrative operations.
 *
 * <p><strong>Use Cases:</strong>
 * <ul>
 *   <li><strong>Reading Existing Batches:</strong> Use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)} to wrap
 *       an existing buffer for reading.</li>
 *   <li><strong>Creating New Batches:</strong> Use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)}
 *       or {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for batch construction.</li>
 *   <li><strong>Log Filtering & Compaction:</strong> Use {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}
 *       to filter records by retention policy during log compaction.</li>
 *   <li><strong>Network I/O:</strong> Use {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel)} or
 *       {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)} for efficient data transmission.</li>
 * </ul>
 *
 * <p><strong>Batch Structure:</strong>
 * The buffer contains a sequence of batches (one or more). Each batch encapsulates records with shared
 * metadata (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.). Batches are decoded lazily as they are
 * iterated; use {@link #batches()} to iterate over all batches or individual methods like
 * {@link #firstBatchSize()} to inspect specific batches.
 *
 * <p><strong>Decompression & Iterator Semantics:</strong>

    /**
     * Peek at the size of the first batch in the buffer without fully parsing it.

     * <p>This method validates the batch header up to and including the magic byte, then reads

     *   <li>Detecting incomplete batches during network reads</li>

    /**
     * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.
     *
     * @param filter                      The filter function
     * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to
     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small
     *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will
     *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the
     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant
     *                                    performance impact.
     * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer
     */

After
    /**
     * Filter records into a destination buffer, applying batch-level and record-level retention policies.
     *
     * <p>This method is the core operation for log compaction and retention enforcement. It iterates
     * over batches and records, applying a filter to determine which batches/records to retain and
     * which to discard. Retained records are re-encoded into the destination buffer.
     *
     * <p><strong>Filtering Semantics:</strong>
     * <ul>
     *   <li><strong>Batch Filtering:</strong> {@link RecordFilter#checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} is called
     *       first to make a fast decision: DELETE (discard entire batch without inspecting records),
     *       RETAIN_EMPTY (keep batch header even if all records are deleted), or DELETE_EMPTY (remove
     *       batch if all records are deleted).</li>
     *   <li><strong>Record Filtering:</strong> For batches not explicitly deleted, {@link RecordFilter#shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)}
     *       is called for each record to determine individual retention.</li>
     *   <li><strong>Batch Rewriting:</strong> If only some records are retained, the batch is re-encoded
     *       with updated offsets and timestamps while preserving base/last offset/sequence for producer
     *       state consistency.</li>
     * </ul>
     *
     * <p><strong>Delete Horizon:</strong>
     * If the batch contains tombstones or transaction markers and the original batch had no delete
     * horizon set, the filter assigns a delete horizon timestamp ({@code filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs})
     * to control when these records are eventually removed.
     *
     * <p><strong>Buffer Overflow Handling:</strong>
     * If the filtered output exceeds the destination buffer's capacity, a l
... (truncated)

    /**
     * Get a duplicate of the backing ByteBuffer for read-only access.
     *
     * <p>Returns a new duplicate with independent position/limit, so modifications to the
     * returned buffer's position do not affect this MemoryRecords' state. The underlying
     * buffer data is shared; any changes to the original buffer's contents will be visible
     * through the returned duplicate.
     *
     * @return A duplicate of the backing ByteBuffer
     */
    public ByteBuffer buffer() {
        return buffer.duplicate();
    }
19. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    @Override
    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {
        if (position < 0)
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid position: " + position + " in read from " + this);
        if (position > buffer.limit())
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Slice from position " + position + " exceeds end position of " + this);
        if (size < 0)
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid size: " + size + " in read from " + this);

        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);
        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.
        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer
        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit
        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the
        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the
        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.
        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();
        slicedBuffer.position(position);
        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);
        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.
        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();

        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);
    }
After
    /**
     * Create a new MemoryRecords instance for a contiguous slice of this buffer.
     *
     * <p>The returned MemoryRecords wraps a view of the same underlying byte data, reducing memory
     * overhead vs. copying. It can be used independently to iterate over a subset of batches.
     * The sliced buffer shares the underlying byte array, so modifications to the original buffer's
     * contents will be visible through the slice.
     *
     * @param position The byte offset within this buffer where the slice should start
     * @param size The maximum number of bytes to include in the slice
     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance backed by a sliced view of the buffer
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if position is negative, exceeds buffer limit, or size is negative
     */
    @Override
    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {
        if (position < 0)
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid position: " + position + " in read from " + this);
        if (position > buffer.limit())
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Slice from position " + position + " exceeds end position of " + this);
        if (size < 0)
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid size: " + size + " in read from " + this);

        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);
        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.
        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer
        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit
        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the
        // slice method 
... (truncated)

    /**
     * Create a builder for constructing a new MemoryRecords instance with default parameters.
     *
     * <p>Convenience method that uses the current (latest) magic version and CREATE_TIME timestamp type.
     * For more control over format and behavior, use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long)}.
     *
     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write records to
     * @param compression The compression type to apply to records (Compression.NONE, Compression.GZIP, etc.)
     * @param timestampType The timestamp type for records (usually CREATE_TIME)
     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in this batch
     * @return A new MemoryRecordsBuilder for appending records
     */
    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,
                                               Compression compression,
                                               TimestampType timestampType,
                                               long baseOffset) {
        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);
    }
21. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,
                                               Compression compression,
                                               TimestampType timestampType,
                                               long baseOffset,
                                               int maxSize) {
        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;
        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)
            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();

        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,
            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,
            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);
    }
After
    /**
     * Create a builder with an explicit maximum size limit.
     *
     * <p>The builder will stop accepting records when the total size reaches maxSize.
     * For LOG_APPEND_TIME timestamp type, the log append time is set to the current system time.
     *
     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write records to
     * @param compression The compression type
     * @param timestampType The timestamp type
     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record
     * @param maxSize The maximum size in bytes this builder is allowed to write
     * @return A new MemoryRecordsBuilder with the size limit
     */
    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,
                                               Compression compression,
                                               TimestampType timestampType,
                                               long baseOffset,
                                               int maxSize) {
        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;
        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)
            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();

        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,
            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,
            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);
    }
22. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {
        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);
    }
After
    /**
     * Create a MemoryRecords instance for reading batches from an existing buffer.
     *
     * <p>The buffer is assumed to contain valid Kafka record batches. Batches are decoded lazily
     * when iterating. The caller retains ownership of the buffer and is responsible for keeping
     * it valid and eventually releasing it.
     *
     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer containing record batches to read
     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance wrapping the buffer
     */
    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {
        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);
    }

    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, records);
    }
After
    /**
     * Create a MemoryRecords instance with the given records in a single batch.
     *
     * <p>Convenience method using default parameters: magic version 2 (current), CREATE_TIME timestamps,
     * and base offset 0. For more control, use one of the other {@code withRecords} variants or
     * {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder}.
     *
     * @param compression The compression type for the batch
     * @param records The records to include (SimpleRecord instances are easier to construct than Record)
     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance containing the records in a single batch
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, records);
    }
24. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,
                baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, false, records);
    }
After
    /**
     * Create a MemoryRecords instance with idempotent producer records.
     *
     * <p>Idempotent batches carry producer ID and base sequence number for deduplication.
     * This is used by producers with exactly-once-per-batch semantics. Base offset is 0
     * and partition leader epoch is unknown (NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH).
     *
     * @param compression The compression type for the batch
     * @param producerId The producer ID
     * @param producerEpoch The producer epoch (for fencing stale producers)
     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number (0 for first batch from producer)
     * @param records The records to include
     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance with idempotent batch semantics
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,
                baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, false, records);
    }
25. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,
                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);
    }
After
    /**
     * Create a MemoryRecords instance with transactional producer records.
     *
     * <p>Transactional batches carry producer ID, epoch, and sequence number, and are marked as
     * part of a transaction. The broker will ensure all-or-nothing delivery and appropriate
     * isolation semantics. Base offset is 0 and partition leader epoch is unknown.
     *
     * @param compression The compression type for the batch
     * @param producerId The producer ID
     * @param producerEpoch The producer epoch
     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number
     * @param records The records to include (will be visible only after transaction commits)
     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance with transactional batch semantics
     * @see #withEndTransactionMarker(long, short, EndTransactionMarker) for committing/aborting the transaction
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,
                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);
    }

    public abstract static class RecordFilter {
        public final long currentTime;

After
    /**
     * Abstract filter for retaining or discarding records during log compaction and retention enforcement.
     *
     * <p><strong>Filtering Two-Level Approach:</strong>
     * <ol>
     *   <li><strong>Batch Level:</strong> {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} makes a fast decision
     *       to DELETE (skip entire batch), RETAIN_EMPTY (keep header only), or proceed to record-level filtering.
     *       This is efficient for large batches where all records share the same fate (e.g., expired segments).</li>
     *   <li><strong>Record Level:</strong> {@link #shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)} is called for each
     *       record in batches that were not DELETE-d, allowing fine-grained retention control.</li>
     * </ol>
     *
     * <p><strong>Common Implementations:</strong>
     * <ul>
     *   <li><strong>Log Compaction:</strong> Delete old versions of compacted keys, retain latest version.</li>
     *   <li><strong>Retention Enforcement:</strong> Delete records older than retention time/size.</li>
     *   <li><strong>Segment Cleanup:</strong> Delete all records in expired segments.</li>
     * </ul>
     *
     * <p><strong>Delete Horizon Semantics:</strong>
     * For records with tombstone values (no value) or transaction abort markers, the filter can set a
     * delete horizon timestamp ({@code currentTime + deleteRetentionMs}) to control when these
     * cleanup records are themselves removed. This delays deletion to allow consumers to see deletions.
     *
     * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong> Not thread-safe; each thread should use its own filter instance.
     */
    public abstract static class RecordFilter {
        /**
         * Current time in milliseconds, used for timestamp-based retention decisions
         * (e.g.,
... (truncated)

    public static class FilterResult {
        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;
        private int messagesRead = 0;
        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from

        private int bytesRead = 0;
        private int messagesRetained = 0;

    /**
     * Result of filtering records through {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}.

     * <p><strong>Metrics Interpretation:</strong>
     * <ul>
     *   <li><strong>messagesRead:</strong> Total records examined (including discarded ones)</li>

     *   <li><strong>bytesRead:</strong> Total bytes examined (all batches, including DELETE-d ones)</li>

     *   <li><strong>maxTimestamp:</strong> Maximum timestamp in retained records</li>

     * </ul>
     *
     * <p>These metrics are useful for monitoring compaction progress and validating log integrity.
     */

28. WRITE /workspace/documentation.md
# Kafka Record Batch Serialization Documentation

## Summary

This document summarizes the comprehensive Javadoc documentation added to Kafka's record batch serialization classes in `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/`.

### Files Modified

1. **RecordBatch.java** (interface)
2. **DefaultRecordBatch.java** (concrete V2+ implementation)
3. **MemoryRecords.java** (in-memory batch collection)

---

## 1. RecordBatch Interface

### Location
`clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java`

### Documentation Added

#### Class-Level Javadoc
- **Purpose**: Comprehensive description of RecordBatch as the abstract interface for Kafka record containers
- **Protocol Context**: Explains the role of batches in the Kafka protocol and message format
- **Format Versions**: Detailed comparison of magic V0/V1 vs. V2+ batches
- **Compaction Behavior**: Explains how base/last offsets and sequences are preserved during log compaction
- **Thread Safety**: Explicit statement that implementations are NOT thread-safe; external synchronization required

#### Method Documentation: `hasProducerId()`
- Purpose: Check if batch has a valid (non-sentinel) producer ID
- Behavior by version: V0/V1 always return false; V2+ check against `NO_PRODUCER_ID`
- Return value semantics clearly documented

### Key Concepts Documented

1. **Record Format Evolution**
   - Magic V0/V1: Single record (uncompressed) or multiple (compressed)
   - Magic V2+: Always support multiple records, producer ID, sequence numbers

2. **Batch Metadata & Deltas**
   - Offset deltas: Relative to batch base offset
   - Timestamp deltas: Relative to batch base timestamp
   - Sequence deltas: Relative to batch base sequence

3. **Producer State Preservation**
   - Base/last offset/sequ
... (truncated)

      

      

        Bash Commands (2)
        
1. $ find /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record -type f -name "*.java" | head -20
2. $ find /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record -name "RecordBatch.java" -o -name "MemoryRecords.java"
